name: Multiply by 2, divide by 6
statement:
You are given an integer $n$. In one move, you can either multiply $n$ by two or divide $n$ by $6$ (if it is divisible by $6$ without the remainder).
Your task is to find the minimum number of moves needed to obtain $1$ from $n$ or determine if it's impossible to do that.
You have to answer $t$ independent test cases.
input:
The first line of the input contains one integer $t$ ($1 \le t \le 2 \cdot 10^4$) — the number of test cases. Then $t$ test cases follow.
The only line of the test case contains one integer $n$ ($1 \le n \le 10^9$).
output: For each test case, print the answer — the minimum number of moves needed to obtain $1$ from $n$ if it's possible to do that or -1 if it's impossible to obtain $1$ from $n$.
